notes: R-mode: All three failure signals were observed (coupon min subtotal bypass, coupon stacking, double-order on same idempotency key). The behavioral success signal (order confirmation page) was also verified.

E-mode: All three risk triggers were executed. Coupon stacking is reproducible with the same coupon code (SAVE10) repeatedly; a different coupon (SAVE20, SAVE5) returned 404 unknown coupon, so only SAVE10 is known valid.
POST /orders is at /orders (API, returns JSON); the HTML form-based order placement is at POST /order (returns HTML confirmation). Idempotency regression is on the /orders API endpoint.

Uncertainty [u-receipt-window]: The confirmation page (POST /order) does not include a receipt URL or expiry information — only an order number is shown. No receipt URL was found to test expiry semantics.

observations.value: REGRESSION [coupon-min-subtotal-bypass]: POST /cart/apply with body {"coupon": "SAVE10", "subtotal": 49} returns HTTP 200 with {"applied": true, "coupon": "SAVE10", "discount": 10, "subtotal": 49, "active": ["SAVE10"]}. Expected: 422 with applied=false. The subtotal minimum predicate (>= 50) is not enforced.

REGRESSION [coupon-stack]: POST /cart/apply accepts a second (and third) application of SAVE10 while it is already active in the cart. Successive calls with {"coupon": "SAVE10", "subtotal": 100} return 200 with active=["SAVE10","SAVE10"] and then active=["SAVE10","SAVE10","SAVE10"]. Only one coupon per cart should be allowed; a 409 with applied=false was expected on the second call.

REGRESSION [idempotent-orders]: POST /orders with the same Idempotency-Key header returns distinct order_ids on every call. First call with Idempotency-Key: test-idem-key-abc123 returned {"order_id": "A1005", "idempotent": false}; second identical call returned {"order_id": "A1006", "idempotent": false}. Similarly, Idempotency-Key: test-idem-key-xyz999 returned A1007 then A1008. Both responses have idempotent=false. This is the double-order regression.

SUCCESS [behavioral]: POST /cart/checkout followed by POST /order with body {"card": "4111111111111111"} returns HTTP 200 with an order confirmation page containing

Order #A1024 confirmed.

. The happy-path checkout flow works.
